question.text: Which of the following statements about Electronic Gold Receipts (EGRs) is CORRECT?

answer.text: They are issued without any backing of physical gold
They are electronic receipts issued on the basis of deposit of underlying physical gold and declared as securities
They are exclusively traded on commodity exchanges
They can only be held by institutional investors

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Electronic Gold Receipts are electronic receipts issued on the basis of deposit of underlying physical gold. They were declared as securities vide gazette notification dated 24 December 2021. **The Trap:** EGRs are backed by physical gold (not unbacked). They are declared as securities under SCRA and can be traded on stock exchanges, not just commodity exchanges. **Cross-Exam Context:** EGRs represent a newer class of securities and their classification is relevant to NISM Series XV and XVI (Commodity Derivatives). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** EGRs provide a regulated way to hold gold electronically with the assurance of physical gold backing. They bridge the gap between physical gold ownership and electronic trading convenience.
